skills.name: Shebang
skills.desc: The shebang is a special line at the beginning of a shell script that specifies the interpreter to be used for executing the script.

skills.name: Comments
skills.desc: Comments in shell scripting are used to add explanations or documentation within the script. They are ignored by the shell and serve as notes for human readers.

skills.name: Quoting Mechanisms
skills.desc: Quoting mechanisms in shell scripting are used to control the interpretation of special characters within strings or variables. Common types include single quotes, double quotes, and backslashes.

skills.name: Variable Declaration
skills.desc: Variable declaration involves creating and initializing variables to store data. In shell scripting, variables are typically assigned values using the '=' operator.

skills.name: Variable Usage
skills.desc: Variable usage refers to the process of accessing and using the values stored in variables within a shell script.

skills.name: String Manipulation
skills.desc: String manipulation involves operations on string variables, such as concatenation, substring extraction, and string length.

skills.name: Arrays
skills.desc: Arrays in shell scripting allow you to store multiple values in a single variable. You can access individual elements using indices.

skills.name: Parameter Expansion
skills.desc: Parameter expansion is a technique for modifying and expanding the values of variables using special operators and patterns.

skills.name: If-Else Statements
skills.desc: If-Else Statements in shell scripting allow you to make decisions based on conditions. You can specify actions to be taken when a condition is true (if) or false (else).

skills.name: Case Statements
skills.desc: Case Statements provide a way to perform different actions based on the value of a variable or expression. They are useful for handling multiple conditions.

skills.name: For Loops
skills.desc: For Loops in shell scripting are used to iterate over a list of items and execute a set of commands for each item in the list.

skills.name: While Loops
skills.desc: While Loops allow you to repeatedly execute a block of commands as long as a specified condition is true.

skills.name: Until Loops
skills.desc: Until Loops are similar to While Loops but execute a block of commands until a specified condition becomes true.

skills.name: Conditional Expressions
skills.desc: Conditional expressions are used to evaluate conditions and return a true or false value, which is often used in If-Else and While Loops.

skills.name: Exit and Return Status
skills.desc: Exit and Return Status refer to the exit code or status returned by a shell script when it finishes executing. It can be used to indicate success or failure.

skills.name: Function Definition
skills.desc: Function definition in shell scripting allows you to define reusable blocks of code with their own local variables and parameters.

skills.name: Arithmetic Operations
skills.desc: Arithmetic operations in shell scripting allow you to perform mathematical calculations using variables and constants.

skills.name: Arithmetic Expansion
skills.desc: Arithmetic expansion involves evaluating mathematical expressions within double parentheses to obtain numeric results.

skills.name: Reading Input
skills.desc: Reading input in shell scripts involves methods for capturing user or file input and using it in the script's logic.

skills.name: Command Substitution
skills.desc: Command substitution allows you to capture the output of a command and use it as part of a shell script's command or expression.

skills.name: Subshells and Command Groups
skills.desc: Subshells and command groups are used to execute commands within a subshell environment or as a group, affecting variable scope and execution context.

skills.name: Advanced Redirection
skills.desc: Advanced redirection techniques in shell scripting involve directing input and output streams to and from files or other processes.

skills.name: Exit Status Checks
skills.desc: Exit status checks involve evaluating the return status of commands or scripts to determine success or failure.

skills.name: Trap Statements
skills.desc: Trap statements in shell scripting are used to set up signal handlers that specify how the script should respond to various signals, such as interrupts or errors.

skills.name: Shell Options and Attributes
skills.desc: Shell options and attributes allow you to configure the behavior and characteristics of the shell environment.

skills.name: Globbing and Pathname Expansion
skills.desc: Globbing and pathname expansion are techniques for matching and expanding file and directory paths using wildcard characters and patterns.
